observation_summary: The HelpFormatter.findWrapPos method throws a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when processing strings that do not contain whitespace, because the loop condition incorrectly allows the index to reach and exceed the string length before calling charAt().
hypothesis: The loop condition 'pos <= text.length()' in the findWrapPos method is an incorrect boundary check. It allows the index 'pos' to equal 'text.length()', which causes 'text.charAt(pos)' to throw an exception. The fix removes this loop entirely, as the logic was attempting to find a whitespace character beyond the desired wrap position, which was both causing the crash and contradicting the intended behavior of the method.
prediction: Removing the while loop that increments 'pos' beyond 'startPos + width' will prevent the index out of bounds error and correctly return the intended wrap position or -1.
experiment_rationale: The fix diff shows the complete removal of the problematic while loop that was performing the out-of-bounds access. This confirms that the logic was not just incorrectly bounded, but fundamentally unnecessary for the intended functionality of the method.
reasoning_summary: The bug is a classic boundary condition error where a loop condition allowed an index to reach an invalid state (text.length()), leading to an exception. While the fix involved removing the loop, the root cause was the incorrect validation logic (the loop condition) that attempted to scan past the end of the string. This falls squarely under 'Checking' as it pertains to incorrect predicate logic in a conditional/loop structure.

alternative_types.why_not_primary: While the loop was part of the algorithm, the specific failure was a boundary check error (the loop condition) that caused an exception. The fix was to remove the incorrect check, which is more characteristic of a Checking defect.
